metadata.title: SceneFlow AI - AI-Powered Video Creation
metadata.description: Transform your ideas into professional videos with AI-powered workflow automation

hero.headline: Envision the Story. SceneFlow Handles the Pipeline.
hero.subheadline: Move past tedious clip-by-clip prompting. Focus entirely on your creative intent with an automated studio built for long-form, multi-language video series.
hero.audienceMicroLine: Built for creators who want results, and teams who want to see how it works.
hero.ctaToolStack: See how it replaces your tool stack
hero.languagePrompt: Hear the hero in your language — same pipeline, new markets
hero.multilangHint: Hero dubs in 7 languages — full pipeline supports 70+ in Production.

pipeline.title: The 3-Pillar Pipeline Architecture
pipeline.subtitle: Three phases from first idea to publish-ready master — Series, Blueprint, and Production.

ui.landscapeHint: Rotate your device for a wider landscape view
ui.howItWorksLabel: How it works in this phase

pillars.title: Series Generation
pillars.header: Build a Universe, Not a Clip
pillars.body: Describe your series or YouTube channel once, and SceneFlow builds the whole world — storyline, cast, and an unlimited episode slate — with a Shared Reference Library that keeps every episode on-brand.

steps.title: Start with your concept
steps.description: Drop in a concept or a detailed description of your series or YouTube channel. SceneFlow turns it into a structured, production-ready foundation — no prompt engineering required.
steps.imagePlaceholder: New series intake — concept or channel description

steps.title: Review the SceneFlow baseline
steps.description: SceneFlow returns a professional baseline scored by Audience Resonance Analysis. Read or listen to the narrated overview, then steer it with your own direction.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Series baseline — Audience Resonance score and narrated overview

steps.title: Create unlimited episodes
steps.description: Spin up as many episodes as your story needs. Each one inherits your series DNA, so the cast, tone, and world stay consistent from episode one to the season finale.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Episode slate — unlimited episodes generated from the series arc

steps.title: Review the Reference Library
steps.description: Browse the shared Reference Library — characters, voices, wardrobe, locations, and props — created and maintained through Production and reused across every episode.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Shared Reference Library — characters, voices, locations, props

steps.title: Direct the Intelligent Assistant Writer
steps.description: Reshape the series or any episode by speaking or typing your direction. The Intelligent Assistant Writer rewrites professional copy instantly — no blank page, no prompt engineering.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Intelligent Assistant Writer — voice or text direction on series and episodes

steps.title: Start production in one click
steps.description: When an episode feels right, start its production with a single click and flow straight into the guided Production pipeline.
steps.imagePlaceholder: One-click start production on a selected episode

pillars.title: Creative Blueprint
pillars.body: Blueprint is your deep film treatment — synopsis, beats, characters, tone, and style — pre-scored by Audience Resonance Analysis so you approve a story that lands before a single frame renders.

steps.title: Review the baseline Blueprint
steps.description: Open a professional baseline Blueprint with Audience Resonance Analysis built in. Read or listen to the narrated overview to hear exactly why it works.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Baseline Blueprint — treatment with Audience Resonance score

steps.title: Direct the Intelligent Assistant Writer
steps.description: Refine the synopsis, beats, characters, and more by speaking or typing your direction. The Intelligent Assistant Writer revises the treatment instantly — no prompt engineering.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Intelligent Assistant Writer — editing synopsis, beats, and characters

steps.title: Review the Reasoning
steps.description: Open the Reasoning section for a creative explanation of the current Blueprint — the story logic behind every beat and character choice.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Reasoning panel — creative explanation of the Blueprint

steps.title: Start production when ready
steps.description: Once the story sings, start production — and the entire pipeline inherits your approved creative DNA with no drift.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Start Production — readiness gate from an approved Blueprint

pillars.title: Guided Production
pillars.body: Production runs concurrent, multi-engine generation — script, audio, frames, and video — with the Intelligent Assistant Director, the Screening Room, and multilanguage streams carrying you from approved script to a published 4K master.

steps.title: Review the baseline script
steps.description: Start from a professional baseline script with Audience Resonance Analysis. Read or listen to the narrated analysis to know what lands before you spend a credit.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Baseline script — Audience Resonance analysis and narrated overview

steps.title: Optimize scene by scene
steps.description: Use the Intelligent Assistant Writer to optimize the script scene by scene — tighten dialogue, reshape beats, and apply one-click improvements by voice or text.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Intelligent Assistant Writer — scene-by-scene script optimization

steps.title: Build the Reference Library
steps.description: Create or update the Reference Library with scripted, custom, or uploaded characters, voices, wardrobes, locations, and props. Edit reference images directly — no "slot machine" regenerations.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Reference Library — characters, voices, wardrobe, locations, props

steps.title: Generate audio
steps.description: Generate dialogue, narration, SFX, and music with Express Production across all scenes, Express Scene, or beat by beat — native-quality voiceover in 70+ languages.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Express audio — all scenes, single scene, or beat by beat

steps.title: Generate and edit frames
steps.description: Generate beat frames with Express Production, Express Scene, or beat by beat, then edit any frame with the Intelligent Assistant Director (IAD) — just describe the change.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Beat frames with Intelligent Assistant Director edits

steps.title: Review the animatic
steps.description: Play the full animatic — frames, dialogue, SFX, and music — in the interactive Screening Room, and share a link for feedback without exporting a file.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Screening Room — interactive animatic review and sharing

steps.title: Generate video
steps.description: Turn approved frames into motion with Express Scene or beat by beat. Retake any shot with the Intelligent Assistant Director — no endless slot-machine regenerations.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Express video generation with IAD retakes

steps.title: Edit in the Mixer
steps.description: Polish every scene in the Mixer — captions, trims, dubbing, overlays, and audio balance — brand-safe finishing without leaving the studio.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Mixer — captions, trim, dubbing, and audio controls

steps.title: Render and review scenes
steps.description: Render your scenes, then review and share them through the Screening Room to lock approval before final assembly.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Rendered scenes reviewed and shared in the Screening Room

steps.title: Generate multilanguage streams
steps.description: Spin up dubbed or lip-synced language streams from the same approved cut — reach 70+ markets without re-shooting a single frame.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Multilanguage streams — dubbed and lip-sync per locale

steps.title: Render final and publish
steps.description: Render the final production into a master and publish — auto-generated thumbnails, titles, and descriptions ready for your YouTube channel and beyond.
steps.imagePlaceholder: Final render and one-click publish to YouTube

screeningRoom.badge: Interactive Screening Room — Animatic Preview Available
screeningRoom.languagePrompt: Hear the pipeline in your target market:

useCasesShowcase.title: Who is SceneFlow For?
useCasesShowcase.subtitle: From concept to a Screening Room animatic to auto-published multilanguage video — one automated pipeline, four ways to ship.
useCasesShowcase.videoLanguagePrompt: Watch this journey in your language

personas.label: The YouTube Creator
personas.headline: From Concept to Cinematic Series — No Prompt Engineering
personas.intro: Start with a concept or a detailed series description. SceneFlow builds the series universe, characters, and unlimited episodes, then uses Express to render audio, frames, and video concurrently. You create and direct with plain text or voice; SceneFlow builds the vision — no prompt engineering, no slot-machine retakes.

story.problem: Making one polished video today usually means paying for and learning half a dozen separate AI apps — one for the script, then others for images, voices, video, music, and editing — and re-generating each one over and over hoping for a usable result. It's costly, technical, and can eat an entire weekend, and your characters still look different from scene to scene.
story.solution: SceneFlow replaces that entire stack with one guided studio. Describe your idea in plain words and it writes the series, designs the cast, and renders the audio, frames, and video together, while a shared Reference Library keeps every character consistent across episodes — no prompt engineering and no re-rolling.
story.outcome: You move from an all-weekend, many-app ordeal to a steady upload rhythm you can actually sustain.

personas.screeningRoomHook: Visualize the story as an animatic before motion video, review scenes before finalizing streams, and share multilanguage streams (70+ languages) for pre-release feedback.
personas.screeningRoomPreview: The Unseen Archive: The Grand Illusion: The Chicago World's Fair Deception

personas.label: The Startup Provider
personas.headline: Run a Global Video Studio — Without Building One
personas.intro: Deliver polished, localized video to small businesses that can't afford an agency. Express renders whole client packages concurrently, Mixer adds brand overlays, watermarks, and timing, and auto-aligned dub/lip-sync ships every deliverable in your client's market language — all from one predictable pipeline.

story.problem: Your small-business clients can't afford a traditional video agency, but producing it yourself means buying and juggling many tools, redoing the work for every client, and manually recreating each video again for every language — costs and hours that make the service hard to scale.
story.solution: SceneFlow gives you one predictable pipeline: it renders a client's whole video package at once, adds each client's branding, overlays, and watermark, and automatically dubs and lip-syncs every video into the customer's market language.
story.outcome: You can run a global, multi-language video studio for many clients without hiring a crew or building infrastructure.

personas.screeningRoomHook: Client-facing review of the animatic, scene sign-off before final streams, and shareable multilanguage previews for stakeholders abroad.
personas.screeningRoomPreview: Main Street Rising: A Local Business Relaunch

personas.label: Enterprise & Institutions
personas.headline: Compliant Training at the Speed of Policy Change
personas.intro: Internal L&D and comms teams ship brand-locked, audit-ready modules without vendor cycles. Refine any frame or line with text or voice to stay on-message, finish with Mixer overlays and watermarks, and roll out auto-aligned dubbed or lip-synced versions to every region from a single approved master.

story.problem: Internal training and communications usually wait weeks on outside vendors and cost a premium — and the moment a policy or product changes, the videos are outdated and off-brand, forcing another expensive round.
story.solution: Teams produce brand-locked, audit-ready videos in-house. Update any line or frame with a typed or spoken instruction, review and approve before paying for the final render, and roll out one approved master to every region.
story.outcome: Training and communications keep pace with policy changes instead of trailing weeks behind.

personas.screeningRoomHook: Review scenes before streams go final and share private Screening Room links for cross-region feedback without exporting unfinished masters.

personas.label: The Educator
personas.headline: Turn Curriculum Into Lessons Students Actually Watch
personas.intro: Large institutions convert dense chapters into localized, narrative video. Express builds full lessons fast, intelligent text/voice edits let faculty tune content without prompt engineering, and auto-aligned dub/lip-sync matches each cohort's language — then auto-publish ships localized titles and thumbnails to every channel.

story.problem: Turning dense course material into engaging video normally takes weeks, specialized skills, or a production budget most institutions don't have — so lessons end up as slideshows students skip, and serving students in multiple languages multiplies the work.
story.solution: SceneFlow builds full narrated lessons quickly, lets faculty adjust content by typing or speaking (no technical prompts), and automatically dubs and lip-syncs each lesson into every cohort's language before publishing with localized titles and thumbnails.
story.outcome: Curriculum becomes video students actually watch — in every campus's language — without a production team.

personas.screeningRoomHook: Faculty preview the animatic before production spend, review lesson scenes, and share streams in 70+ languages for multi-campus feedback.
personas.screeningRoomPreview: Renaissance Workshop: Seeing the Past in Motion

keyFeatures.title: The Features That Set SceneFlow Apart
keyFeatures.subtitle: Beyond the pipeline — intelligent assistants, express production, and global distribution tools that turn SceneFlow into a complete studio, not another AI tab.

features.title: Intelligent Assistant Writer
features.description: Never start from a blank page. SceneFlow builds professional Series, Blueprint, and Script copy from a concept or detailed outline. Direct the baseline with plain speech or typed direction — no complex prompt engineering.

learnMore.problem: The blank page is where most projects stall. Generic AI writing tools spit out bland, one-size-fits-all copy that ignores who you are actually talking to — so you spend hours rewriting, or worse, ship something that never connects with your audience.
learnMore.solution: The Intelligent Assistant Writer fuses your defined target audience with proven screenwriting and copywriting technique to draft on-brief Series, Blueprint, and Script copy. Steer every beat by plain voice or text — no prompt engineering, no rewriting from scratch.
learnMore.outcome: Audience-tuned, professional-grade writing in minutes, not hours or days — so you start every project with momentum instead of a blinking cursor.

features.title: Audience Resonance Analysis (ARA)
features.description: Define your target audience and get expert analysis with a score, detailed recommendations, and one-click revisions — across Series, Blueprint, and Script before you spend on production.

learnMore.problem: By the time you find out a story misses the mark, you have already burned hours and credits rendering audio, frames, and video. Creative guesswork is expensive, and reshoots are even more so.
learnMore.solution: ARA is a powerful engine that runs a test-audience screening on your copy before a single audio clip, frame, or video is generated. Get a resonance score, pinpointed recommendations, and one-click revisions across Series, Blueprint, and Script.
learnMore.outcome: De-risk every project up front — greenlight only the stories that will land, and stop paying to render ideas that were never going to work.

features.title: Reference Library
features.description: Create consistent character, wardrobe, voice, location, and prop references across Series. Use scripted characters or build custom ones with auto voice generation based on character image and role.

learnMore.problem: AI video's dirty secret is drift: your hero's face, wardrobe, and settings mutate shot to shot and episode to episode, shattering the illusion and forcing endless re-rolls to stay on-model.
learnMore.solution: Build AI-assisted or fully custom characters, wardrobes, voices, locations, and props once, then reuse them everywhere. Every generation pulls from the same locked references — with auto voice generation matched to each character's image and role.
learnMore.outcome: Perfect consistency across scenes, productions, and entire series — zero character drift, so your audience stays immersed in the story instead of noticing the seams.

features.title: Intelligent Workflow Assistant
features.description: SceneFlow is intuitively designed and guides you through script, Reference Library, audio, and visual production — with step-by-step checklists and the Cue AI assistant at every milestone.

learnMore.problem: Professional production tools are powerful but punishing — steep learning curves, easy-to-miss steps, and hours of tutorials before you produce anything worth watching.
learnMore.solution: SceneFlow guides you through the automated pipeline step by step, with guardrails that keep you on track — like completing your Reference Library before you generate frames. Click Start and let SceneFlow lead the way.
learnMore.outcome: Go from sign-up to shipping without a single tutorial. The right next step is always obvious, so nothing gets skipped and nothing gets wasted.

features.title: Express Production
features.description: Generate frames and videos in minutes, not hours, with SceneFlow's concurrent processing engine — Express orchestrates audio, image, and video generation in parallel across your scenes.

learnMore.problem: Generating a production one asset at a time turns a single project into an all-day wait — you queue a clip, watch it render, then repeat hundreds of times while momentum evaporates.
learnMore.solution: Express Production taps SceneFlow's dedicated cloud to run concurrent generations of audio, frames, and video across your entire production at once, instead of one slow job after another.
learnMore.outcome: A 30-beat production finishes in about 30 minutes instead of roughly 2.5 hours — so you iterate the same day and ship while the idea is still hot.

features.title: Intelligent Assistant Director (IAD)
features.description: No endless slot-machine generations. Direct frame or video revisions by speech or typed direction — remove the coffee mug from the table and IAD handles the rest, using your Reference Library for consistency.

learnMore.problem: Most AI tools force you to re-roll the entire shot and pray when you want one small change — a slot machine that wastes credits and rarely gives you exactly what you pictured.
learnMore.solution: IAD lets you direct precise frame or video revisions in plain speech or text — 'remove the coffee mug from the table' — and applies the edit while honoring your Reference Library for continuity.
learnMore.outcome: Get the exact shot you envisioned with surgical edits instead of random regenerations — less wasted spend, more creative control, faster final cuts.

features.title: Multilanguage Streams
features.description: 70+ languages. Express-generate audio in minutes, auto-aligned to animatic beat frames and video. Generate dubbed or lip-sync language streams so every market hears your story natively.

learnMore.problem: Reaching a global audience usually means re-recording voice talent, re-timing every scene, and rebuilding the project once per language — a cost and coordination nightmare that keeps most creators single-market.
learnMore.solution: Express-generate audio in 70+ languages in minutes, auto-aligned to your animatic beat frames and video. Produce dubbed or lip-synced streams from the same approved cut, no re-shoots required.
learnMore.outcome: Ship every story natively to 70+ markets from one production — multiply your reach and revenue without multiplying your workload.

features.title: Version Control
features.description: Generate and manage multiple versions of each production stream. Compare takes, pin favorites, and iterate without losing prior work — every scene keeps its full version history.

learnMore.problem: Creative iteration is messy: the best take gets overwritten, alternates vanish, and you can never quite get back to the version everyone loved two edits ago.
learnMore.solution: Version Control keeps every generated take for each stream. Compare versions side by side, pin favorites, and keep iterating — the full history of every scene stays intact.
learnMore.outcome: Explore boldly without fear of losing your best work — always recover the perfect take and make confident final decisions.

features.title: Screening Room
features.description: Review and share Animatic (scene frames with dialogue, SFX, and music), Video (scene video with full audio mix), and Streams (each language produced). Viewers get playback controls; reviewers can leave feedback.

learnMore.problem: Getting sign-off usually means exporting rough files, emailing them around, and stitching together scattered feedback from a dozen threads — slow, messy, and easy to lose track of.
learnMore.solution: The Screening Room lets you review and share Animatics, full-audio Video, and per-language Streams from one link. Viewers get real playback controls; reviewers leave structured feedback right on the cut.
learnMore.outcome: Lock approvals faster with stakeholder feedback in one place — no premature exports, no lost notes, no version confusion.

features.title: Auto Publish
features.description: Auto-create thumbnails, titles, descriptions, and multilanguage audio tracks for one-click YouTube publishing. Reach billions of viewers with distribution-ready output from your studio.

learnMore.problem: The work is not done when the video is — thumbnails, titles, descriptions, and language tracks are their own time sink standing between your finished cut and a live upload.
learnMore.solution: Auto Publish generates thumbnails, titles, descriptions, and multilanguage audio tracks, then pushes to YouTube in one click — distribution-ready output straight from your studio.
learnMore.outcome: Go from final render to published in minutes, reaching billions of viewers across languages without a separate packaging-and-upload marathon.

features.title: Bring Your Own Key (BYOK)
features.description: Provide your personal or corporate API key for image and video processing. Reduce platform costs with Bring Your Own Key for supported providers while keeping full studio control.

learnMore.problem: As you scale, per-generation platform fees stack up — and you have little control over the underlying model costs eating into every production's margin.
learnMore.solution: BYOK lets you plug in your personal or corporate API key for image and video processing with supported providers, so generation runs on your own account while you keep the full SceneFlow studio experience.
learnMore.outcome: Cut platform costs and control your spend at scale — same guided pipeline, your economics.

features.title: Production Budget Management
features.description: Calculate and track each production's credit budget. Get guidance on reducing costs — draft frames, fewer video generations — and purchase add-on credits when you need more runway.

learnMore.problem: Creative projects are notorious for silent budget overruns — you only discover you have blown through your credits after the fact, mid-production and out of runway.
learnMore.solution: Production Budget Management calculates and tracks each production's credit budget in real time, with guidance on trimming costs — draft frames, fewer video generations — and add-on credits whenever you need more headroom.
learnMore.outcome: Stay in full control of spend from first beat to final cut — no surprise overruns, no stalled productions, predictable costs every time.

audiencePaths.prompt: Who are you? Pick your path

automate.label: Automate it
automate.description: Guided workflow — approve pre-vis, ship faster

paths.label: Creator
paths.outcome: Ship on schedule — stop rebuilding in every tool
paths.narrative: You have the story, but every episode means re-exporting assets, re-prompting in new tabs, and hoping continuity holds. SceneFlow connects Series, Blueprint, and Production in one studio — so you approve the look in pre-vis, generate scene by scene, and ship consistent episodes on schedule instead of rebuilding from scratch.

example.challengeDescription: You have a strong script, but learning six separate generation and edit tools — or getting stuck in slot-machine video loops — slows every upload. Serialized YouTube hits and YouTube TV drama cadences demand faster turnaround than fragmented AI tabs can deliver.
example.solutionDescription: Ship episodic series and scroll-stopping shorts from one studio — beat-first pre-vis, Reference Library continuity, and publish-ready masters for the YouTube-led creator economy.
example.win: Move from occasional uploads to a reliable production cadence with less overhead.

paths.label: In-house team
paths.outcome: Replace slow vendor cycles — control brand and budget
paths.narrative: Internal video requests pile up while agency quotes stretch timelines and brand guidelines drift in every revision round. SceneFlow puts L&D, comms, and product explainers in one guided workflow — your team owns the script, pre-vis, and master output without renting a studio or waiting on vendors.

example.challengeDescription: Comms, L&D, and marketing teams wait weeks for agency or vendor video — or face an overwhelming tool stack with no repeatable in-house system. Every path brings inconsistent branding and stalled campaigns.
example.solutionDescription: Assign staff to produce video on a predictable credit budget — with brand templates, approval before final render, and no crew days or open-ended edit cycles.
example.win: Ship training, comms, and campaign video on your timeline — not the vendor's backlog.

paths.label: Production shop
paths.outcome: Productize a niche with repeatable intake → delivery
paths.narrative: Custom video work is profitable until every client becomes a one-off — new tools, new handoffs, and margins that vanish in rework. SceneFlow turns your niche into a product: repeatable intake, white-label templates, voice-cloned narration, and a clear path from brief to delivered MP4.

example.challengeDescription: Memoir, legacy, real estate, and education clients need fast turnaround — but every project starts from scratch. Per-deliverable handoffs plus slot-machine video re-rolls across script, image, voice, video, music, and edit tools eat margin on volume.
example.solutionDescription: Build repeatable workflows for memoir studios and niche verticals. Template uploads, voice clones, avatars, and pre-vis approval — customize per client and grow margin on volume.
example.win: Launch a video memoir or niche studio service with repeatable delivery — not a one-off freelance grind.

paths.label: Agency
paths.outcome: Faster pitches and scalable client delivery
paths.narrative: Winning the pitch means showing the idea — not describing it — but pre-viz and localization eat the margin before the contract is signed. SceneFlow gets client-ready pre-viz in front of stakeholders fast, then scales delivery across campaigns and 70+ languages without rebuilding the pipeline per account.

example.challengeDescription: Winning the pitch is only half the battle. Recurring client work needs fast turnaround — but per-deliverable handoffs and slot-machine video re-rolls across separate generation and edit tools slow every pitch cycle.
example.solutionDescription: Move from pitch spec to recurring delivery with brand-safe controls, client review before final render, and predictable credit costs per project.
example.win: Deliver faster pitch cycles and recurring client work with less production risk and more margin.

paths.label: Film Production
paths.outcome: Script to an interactive animatic you can screen and test before you shoot
paths.narrative: Previz and audience testing used to mean weeks of storyboards, temp VO, and expensive guesswork before cameras roll. SceneFlow takes your script to an interactive animatic with Express pre-vis — optimize with Audience Resonance, screen it in the Screening Room, and commit budget only when the story lands.

example.challengeTitle: Slow Pre-Vis Before You Can Test the Story
example.challengeDescription: You have a script — but table reads, storyboard cycles, and fragmented previz tools delay investor pitches, audience tests, and greenlight decisions.
example.solutionDescription: Upload your script (MDX, FDX, and other formats), optimize with Audience Resonance, and generate an interactive pre-vis to screen and test — with Express concurrent generation across beats, in 70+ language options, and fast multi-language overlays.
example.win: Validate story and audience fit before principal photography — with a shareable animatic, not a weeks-long previz cycle.

whySceneFlow.title: Why SceneFlow vs prompt-and-generate tools
whySceneFlow.subtitle: Gemini Studio and Google Flow excel at clips. SceneFlow bundles the full production workflow.

rows.us: Structured phases with editable baselines at each step
Reference Library + Beat Frames lock visuals before video
Audience Resonance before render; Screening Room before publish
Script → streams → assembly → distribution in one app

beatFirstPipeline.title: Beat-First Video Pipeline
beatFirstPipeline.subtitle: Approve before you render — not slot-machine regeneration
beatFirstPipeline.intro: No guess-and-check generations. You preview and approve the look before SceneFlow spends credits on final video.
beatFirstPipeline.description: SceneFlow structures visual approval at every step: Express Pre-vis for review, Beat Frames to lock composition, then F2V per beat. You refine the look before burning credits on drift and hallucinations.
steps: Express Pre-vis — share audio-visual beats for review in minutes
Beat Frames — lock start/end composition with Reference Library continuity
F2V per beat — generate video from approved frames, not blind prompts
Continuous EXT chain — native +7s extension steps for long dialogue beats
Production Mixer — timing, audio, and stream export to Screening Room
beatFirstPipeline.footerLine: Fewer slot-machine regenerations — approve visuals before final video spend

trustSafeguard.title: Layered Guardrails for
trustSafeguard.subtitle: Google-native safety on every generation, Extended Creative Services with Guardrails when policy limits apply, and additional moderation and risk mitigation across your Studio workflow.

tiers.title: Google-Native Safety
tiers.description: Every image and video generation runs through Google Vertex AI safety filters with thresholds tuned for professional creative workflows—not consumer-grade defaults.
highlights: Vertex Responsible AI on Veo, Imagen, and Gemini generation
Production-tuned harm thresholds for cinematic content
Your creative assets are not used to train shared models

tiers.title: Extended Guardrails
tiers.description: When the primary path is blocked by policy, Extended Creative Services with Guardrails can complete delivery—only after an additional content review pass before storage.
highlights: Prompt adjustment and method retries on the primary Google path
Guarded fallback path with mandatory pre-storage review
Same content standards across primary and extended paths

tiers.title: Validation & Provenance
tiers.description: Additional moderation and risk mitigation is available across Blueprint, script, pre-vis, and segment video—using the same credit model as other Studio tools. Successful clips receive signed content provenance records.
highlights: Content validation signals before heavy render spend
Signed content hash and provenance metadata on segment video
Audit logs, violation tracking, and abuse reporting for platform protection

trustSafeguard.morNote: SceneFlow operates shared responsibility with our Merchant of Record: provider-scale safety from Google, platform guardrails and audit trails from SceneFlow—reducing risk for creators and the platform alike.

extendedScenes.title: Beyond the 8-Second Clip
extendedScenes.subtitle: Break past the single-clip wall—approve the look first, then extend your scene step by step into seamless monologues, explainers, and dialogue beats.
extendedScenes.intro: SceneFlow does not promise one unlimited render. It plans long beats as ordered sequences: an initial clip up to 8 seconds, then continuous +7 second extension steps—with beat-first approval before you spend credits.

steps.title: 8s initial clip
steps.description: Frame-to-video or image-to-video from approved Beat Frames locks composition before motion.

steps.title: +7s extension steps
steps.description: Each step continues the same angle and motion—ideal for uninterrupted dialogue within a beat.

steps.title: Beat-first approval
steps.description: Long lines auto-split at pre-vis review. You approve frames before the chain renders—not slot-machine regeneration.

steps.title: Production Mixer finish
steps.description: Chain segments stitch into scene playback; elastic audio holds cover narration that runs longer than video.

extendedScenes.footnote: Extension chains require the primary Google generation path and valid video references between steps (references expire after approximately 48 hours). Each API step remains within Veo duration limits—the total beat length comes from chaining, not a single long render.

creativeRange.title: Every Style, Every Screen
creativeRange.titleAccent: Your creative DNA, locked in Blueprint
creativeRange.subtitle: Blueprint is where your creative DNA locks in—art style, aspect ratio, tone, and core narrative. SceneFlow feeds those choices into treatment, Audience Resonance, and script generation so scene directions match your frame and your look from beat one—not as last-minute export settings.

pillars.title: Form Follows Function (and Framing)
pillars.description: Screen size dictates visual storytelling. A 9:16 vertical demands tighter blocking, faster hooks, and close-up-heavy direction—nothing like 16:9 widescreen staging. Lock aspect ratio in Blueprint so script generation writes scene directions that match the frame.

pillars.title: Art Style Dictates the Prose
pillars.description: Noir anime and Pixar 3D need different dialogue, atmosphere, and pacing. Choose your look in Blueprint and the LLM weaves that texture into the script's DNA—not as a filter applied after the fact.

creativeRange.blueprintTagline: Tone and core narrative complete the lock—Blueprint is the brain; Production is the muscle.

artStyles.subsectionIntro: Your look is not a render preset—it is the voice of the script. Lock art style in Blueprint and every beat inherits that aesthetic.
artStyles.footnote: Locked in Blueprint Visual Foundation—carried through script, pre-vis, and final render.

items.name: Photorealistic
items.description: Professional photography style
items.marketingBody: Photorealism delivers the mainstream appeal prestige TV and documentary audiences expect. Lock realistic characters and lighting across chained beats so episodic drama and docuseries maintain emotional continuity from opener to finale.

items.name: Anime (90s)
items.description: Classic 90s anime style
items.marketingBody: Anime-style cel shading carries deep fan engagement across multi-episode web series and longform narrative arcs. Generative engines trained on this medium yield crisp line art and predictable framing — ideal for serialized production at scale.

items.name: Pixar Animation
items.description: 3D animated Pixar style
items.marketingBody: Soft, rounded geometry and expressive character design create emotional warmth that sustains viewers across a full season. Perfect for animated series, family longform, and branded storytelling where character consistency matters beat to beat.

items.name: Concept Art
items.description: Game/film concept art
items.tagline: Film and series pre-vis keyframes with moody, production-ready lighting.

items.name: Ghibli-esque
items.description: Studio Ghibli inspired
items.marketingBody: This style taps directly into a major creative movement prioritizing "Craft as Luxury." Watercolor textures and painterly backgrounds build handcrafted episodic worlds where organic imperfection reads as deliberate artistry across a longform series.

items.name: Comic Book
items.description: Comic book ink style
items.tagline: Bold ink, halftone texture, and pop-art color for multi-episode serial storytelling.

items.name: Oil Painting
items.description: Classical oil painting
items.tagline: Classical brushwork and rich pigment for documentary and historical longform.

items.name: Digital Art
items.description: Modern digital illustration
items.tagline: Stylized illustration for animated documentary and episodic explainer series.

items.name: Watercolor
items.description: Soft watercolor painting
items.tagline: Soft pigment washes for memoir, documentary, and contemplative longform.

items.name: Sketch/Line Art
items.description: Pencil sketch style
items.tagline: Detailed linework and pencil texture for pre-vis-to-production longform beats.

outputFormats.subsectionIntro: Framing is a storytelling choice, not an export checkbox. Blueprint assumes your aspect ratio before the first scene direction is written.
outputFormats.resolutionFootnote: Chosen in Blueprint; script and scene direction assume this frame. Export at 720p or 1080p where your project supports it.

items.label: 16:9 Widescreen
items.description: YouTube main, connected TV, and episodic series—Blueprint writes widescreen scene direction and ensemble blocking for cinematic staging.

items.label: 9:16 Vertical
items.description: Vertical episodic drama and mobile-first series—Blueprint writes vertical-first blocking, tighter pacing, and close-up-heavy direction binge audiences expect.

items.label: 1:1 Square
items.description: Square episode clips, interview compilations, and chapter highlights pulled from a longform series — one aspect for feed-native episode libraries.

items.label: 4:3 Classic
items.description: Training modules, documentary archives, and presentation-friendly frames when your audience expects a classic aspect for longform delivery.

toolStack.title: The Broken Way vs the SceneFlow Way
toolStack.subtitle: Experienced producers: stop losing hours to copy-paste across script, image, voice, video, music, and edit tools. New to AI video: one guided studio automates the stack so you ship without learning six separate apps.

brokenWay.title: The Broken Way

tools.name: Image generation
tools.category: Frames and stills in a separate tool

tools.name: Video generation
tools.category: Slot-machine re-rolls — prompt, generate, reject, repeat

tools.name: Video editing
tools.category: Manual assembly, export, and upscaling polish

brokenWay.chaosLabel: Six tabs. Re-roll until something sticks.
painPoints: Copy-paste between generation and edit tools
Slot-machine video generation — re-roll until something sticks
No audience check before you spend

sceneflowWay.title: The SceneFlow Way
sceneflowWay.tagline: One studio. Guided automation — or serious throughput if you already know the stack.
sceneflowWay.syncLabel: One project file — no tab juggling
benefits: Guided phases — stop tab juggling and context loss
Pre-vis approval — stop slot-machine re-rolls before video spend
Audience Resonance score before heavy render
Publish-ready master in 30–60 min, not 4+ hrs

toolStack.comparisonCaption: One studio replaces fragmented generation tools, slot-machine video re-rolls, and manual edit assembly
toolStack.comparisonImageAlt: Traditional production overhead vs SceneFlow automated studio — faster concept to publish-ready video
toolStack.costNote: Same AI spend, less wasted time. You pay for models either way — through SceneFlow credits, your own provider account, or BYOK. SceneFlow removes the copy-paste assembly tax so you can ship more videos with the same budget.
toolStack.proofLine: Validated on a residential listing tour: full manual stack 4+ hours → same deliverable in SceneFlow under an hour.

comparison.title: Production speed without the overhead
comparison.subtitle: Replace fragmented prompt tools and manual assembly with one automated studio — from first concept to publish-ready master.
comparison.caption: One studio replaces fragmented prompt tools, manual edits, and multi-platform handoffs
comparison.imageAlt: Traditional production overhead vs SceneFlow automated studio — faster concept to publish-ready video

howItWorks.title: From concept to publish-ready video
howItWorks.subtitle: Series is optional. The core path is Blueprint → Production → Screening Room — pre-vis, animatic, scene video, and master MP4 along the way.
howItWorks.tagline: Every step is expertly automated. Every step gives you full control.
howItWorks.readyTitle: Ready to test the full pipeline?

Auto-generate episode outlines across the season
Sync into Blueprint and Production per episode
keyFeatures: Define your universe once — characters, tone, and season arc
Cohesive episode outlines — auto-generated across the season

phases.subtitle: Story Development

keyFeatures: Generate → Review → Iterate → Start Production workflow
Concept to structure in minutes — logline, beats, characters, and tone
Audience Resonance scoring with 80+ target before Production
Collaborator share links — feedback synthesis into guided revision

phases.subtitle: Script to Screening Room
phases.description: Script and Shoot tabs guide Foundation → Pre-Vis → Footage → Mixer → Streams. Scene-by-scene checkpoints minimize redos; Screening Room handles preview, assembly, and publishing.
keySteps: Lock script, voices, and scene audio
Pre-Vis scene by scene — gallery and share for review
Shoot tab — Footage → Mixer → Streams per scene
Screening Room — preview, assemble master, publish
keyFeatures: Foundation — script, voices, scene audio, Audience Resonance
Pre-Vis — direction, audio, and beat frames per scene with share links
Shoot — Footage, Mixer, and Streams without leaving Production
Screening Room — scene preview, master assembly, screenings, and YouTube publish

phases.subtitle: Preview · Assemble · Publish
phases.description: One finishing surface inside Production: preview scene videos, stitch streams into a master MP4, run shareable screenings with analytics, and publish to YouTube or export bundles.
keySteps: Preview rendered scene streams in sequence
Assemble master — pick Animatic or Video per scene
Review insights and publish to YouTube
keyFeatures: Scene-video playback — mirrors Pre-Vis player UX with rendered streams
Assembly presets — All Video, Hybrid, or custom per-scene mix
Shareable screenings — /s/ links with scoring and structured feedback
YouTube wizard, short-form exports, and distribution bundles

simpleWalkthrough.title: From concept to publish-ready video
simpleWalkthrough.subtitle: Start with an idea or script, optimize with Blueprint and Audience Resonance, visualize with Pre-Vis, shoot scene video, test in Screening Room, and publish — all in one studio.
simpleWalkthrough.tagline: Every step is expertly automated. Every step gives you full control.
simpleWalkthrough.readyTitle: Ready to test the full pipeline?

steps.stepLabel: Start with your Concept or Existing Script
steps.shortDescription: Begin with a natural-language concept or import your screenplay — Fountain, Final Draft, or paste your script.
steps.detailedDescription: SceneFlow accepts ideas in plain language or structured script formats. The AI helps expand concepts into treatments and can import existing screenplays so you never start from a blank page.
steps.screenshotSlot: Insert screenshot: New project concept input or script import dialog

steps.shortDescription: Transform ideas into a structured treatment — logline, beats, characters, and tone — before heavy rendering.
steps.detailedDescription: Blueprint automatically crafts a comprehensive story outline. Refine character arcs, plot beats, and narrative tone with intelligent suggestions. Collaborator share links let stakeholders review before Production.

steps.stepLabel: Optimize your script with Audience Resonance
steps.shortDescription: Score your script for target-audience fit and apply scene-by-scene recommendations with one-click fixes.
steps.detailedDescription: Audience Resonance (AR) critically analyzes your script against the audience you define. Get section-level scores, actionable recommendations, and Guided Edits — auto-apply fixes per scene or make global script changes from the AR dialog.

steps.shortDescription: Generate dialogue audio and beat frames scene by scene. Share animatics for approval before final video spend.
steps.detailedDescription: Pre-Vis Studio generates direction, audio, and beat frames per scene — checkpoints that minimize costly redos when characters or tone change. Share the player for collaboration; the full animatic can be your final deliverable for many projects.

steps.shortDescription: Generate beat-level video, mix audio and overlays, and export full scene streams — Footage, Mixer, and Streams in one Shoot tab.
steps.detailedDescription: The Shoot tab in Production is your on-set: generate video from approved Pre-Vis frames (Footage), fine-tune timing, watermarks, and audio in the Mixer, then render complete scene MP4s in Streams.
subPoints: Footage — beat-by-beat video generation from Pre-Vis start and end frames
Mixer — watermark, text overlay, and audio timing per scene
Streams — full rendered scene video (all beats) ready for Screening Room

steps.stepLabel: Test your production with Screening Room
steps.shortDescription: Preview scene videos in sequence, assemble a master MP4, and run shareable screenings with structured feedback.
steps.detailedDescription: Screening Room mirrors the Pre-Vis player UX but plays rendered scene streams. Pick Animatic or Video per scene, stitch a master, share /s/ review links, and triage timestamped feedback before publish.

steps.shortDescription: Distribute your master to YouTube, export short-form cuts, and track audience insights from one finishing surface.
steps.detailedDescription: From Screening Room, run the YouTube publishing wizard, create social short-form exports, and download distribution bundles. Analytics and reviewer feedback stay in one place — no handoff to separate tools.

useCases.title: Whatever Video You Can Imagine,
useCases.subtitle: Entertainment and creator series, real-estate showcases, education, podcasts, news formats, and branded campaigns — one automated studio from concept to publish-ready master.
useCases.qualifyingStatement: SceneFlow turns your source photos, uploads, and descriptions into narrated walkthrough videos — with optional voice and character presence you control. These are AI-altered presentations: realistic visualizations grounded in your materials, not live camera recordings. Review and approve every pre-vis before publish; label outputs clearly so audiences know what they're watching.

challenge.title: The "B-Roll" Bottleneck
challenge.description: You have a strong script, but learning six separate generation and edit tools — or getting stuck in slot-machine video loops — slows every upload. Serialized YouTube hits and YouTube TV drama cadences demand faster turnaround than fragmented AI tabs can deliver.

solution.title: The Automated Storyteller
solution.description: Ship episodic series and scroll-stopping shorts from one studio — beat-first pre-vis, Reference Library continuity, and publish-ready masters for the YouTube-led creator economy.

Scale to episode series with a shared reference library

creator.win: Move from occasional uploads to a reliable production cadence with less overhead.

categories.title: Entertainment & Creator Series
categories.qualifyingStatement: YouTube now rivals traditional TV for attention—indie animation studios and solo creators ship serialized hits with tens of millions of views per episode. SceneFlow gives you beat-first pre-vis, Reference Library continuity, and publish-ready masters for the creator economy—not slot-machine clip tools.

examples.label: YouTube TV Drama
examples.description: Imagine shipping 16:9 episodic drama on YouTube—where YouTube now holds 13.4% of U.S. TV viewing and over 35% of watch time happens on connected TV. SceneFlow helps creators grow that share with engaging alternative content: beat-first pre-vis, Reference Library continuity, and publish-ready widescreen masters.

examples.label: Animated Web Series
examples.description: Imagine locking stylized characters in your Reference Library once—then shipping serialized episodes in anime, Pixar, or Ghibli-inspired art with the consistency indie hits like Glitch Productions demand.

examples.label: Episodic YouTube Series
examples.description: Imagine auto-generated season arcs in Series Studio—episode outlines sync into Blueprint and Production so every face, voice, and beat stays aligned across a growing channel.

examples.label: Creator Reality & Competition
examples.description: Imagine producing reality-scale creator competitions without a broadcast crew—multi-cam beats, stakeholder Screening Room reviews, and same-week turnaround from script to master MP4.

examples.label: Vertical Mobile Drama
examples.description: Imagine photoreal 9:16 hooks and serialized mobile episodes built for scroll—approve Beat Frames before render, chain native extensions for emotional beats that stop the thumb. SceneFlow outputs vertical masters ready for YouTube Shorts and mobile feeds.

categories.title: Property, Spaces & Hospitality
categories.qualifyingStatement: Built from your real property photos — presented as a narrated AI-altered walkthrough with your voice and avatar. A realistic preview of layout and features, not a substitute for an in-person showing. All tours are clearly labeled.

examples.label: Residential Real Estate
examples.description: Imagine uploading your property images with your saved character reference and voice. Then instantly generating a professional listing walkthrough with your image and voice — in over 70 languages.

examples.label: Commercial Real Estate
examples.description: Imagine uploading floor plans, renderings, and neighborhood data with your broker's saved voice and avatar. Then instantly producing investor pitch walkthroughs that bring every square foot to life — localized in 70+ languages.

examples.label: Short-Term Rentals
examples.description: Imagine uploading house photos and amenity notes with your host's saved voice. Then instantly publishing digital welcome videos that greet every guest in their language — before they ever check in.

examples.label: Hospitality & Tourism
examples.description: Imagine uploading hotel photos and itinerary highlights with your brand's saved host voice and avatar. Then instantly shipping virtual tours and narrated travel guides in 70+ languages — no film crew per property.

examples.label: Museum & Gallery Guides
examples.description: Imagine uploading exhibit photos and curator notes as displays change. Then instantly refreshing narrated gallery tours in 70+ languages — no reshoot when the exhibition rotates.

examples.label: K-12 & Higher Ed
examples.description: Imagine uploading lesson materials with your instructor's saved voice and character reference. Then instantly generating full curriculum modules your ESL and global campus students can watch in 70+ languages.

examples.label: Corporate L&D
examples.description: Imagine uploading training slides with your L&D lead's saved voice. Then instantly publishing module videos—chain native +7 second extensions for long explainers, approve Beat Frames before render.

examples.label: Software SaaS Tutorials
examples.description: Imagine uploading UI screenshots as reference frames with your product expert's saved voice. Then instantly generating walkthrough videos that animate every click — updated the day your UI ships.

examples.label: Niche Skill Tutoring
examples.description: Imagine uploading step photos with your instructor's saved voice and character reference. Then instantly publishing professional how-to series — cooking, DIY, or certification prep — in 70+ languages.

examples.label: Medical/Patient Education
examples.description: Imagine uploading procedure diagrams with your clinician's approved saved voice. Then instantly generating clear patient education videos families can understand in 70+ languages.

examples.label: Video Memoirs
examples.description: Imagine uploading family photos, interview audio, and scene notes with a saved narrator voice. Then instantly shaping chapter-based memoir videos — approve the pre-vis before final render.

examples.label: Hyper-Local News
examples.description: Imagine uploading today's photos and bulletins with your anchor's saved voice. Then instantly publishing a daily neighborhood news brief — no film crew, no missed deadline.

examples.label: Financial & Market Recaps
examples.description: Imagine uploading market data and chart snapshots each morning with your analyst's saved voice. Then instantly turning them into a narrated visual digest — ready before the opening bell.

examples.label: Sports Commentary
examples.description: Imagine uploading game stats and still photography with your commentator's saved voice. Then instantly generating recap videos with animated action — published while fans are still talking.

examples.label: True Crime & Historical Docs
examples.description: Imagine locking historical figures in your Reference Library once. Then instantly producing multi-part episodes where every face and voice stays consistent — series after series.

examples.label: Weather & Emergency Alerts
examples.description: Imagine uploading emergency bulletins with your agency's trusted saved voice. Then instantly broadcasting clear alerts in 70+ languages across social platforms — in minutes, not days.

examples.label: Product Explainer Videos
examples.description: Imagine uploading product catalog shots with your brand's saved presenter voice and avatar. Then instantly generating a cinematic explainer series — approve the pre-vis before you render.

examples.label: Case Study/Testimonials
examples.description: Imagine uploading client headshots, project photos, and success metrics with a saved narrator voice. Then instantly producing polished visual case studies — without a testimonial shoot.

examples.label: Recruitment & Branding
examples.description: Imagine uploading office photos and culture highlights with your recruiter's saved voice and avatar. Then instantly giving candidates a narrated day-in-the-life tour — in 70+ languages for global hiring.

examples.label: Conference & Event Promos
examples.description: Imagine uploading speaker bios, session details, and venue photos with your event host's saved voice. Then instantly generating speaker intros and what-to-expect guides — refreshed every time the agenda changes.

examples.label: NGO Impact Reports
examples.description: Imagine uploading field photography and impact data with your organization's saved narrator voice. Then instantly turning donor reports into emotive narrated videos — your mission's voice, not a slideshow.

examples.label: Public Health Announcements
examples.description: Imagine uploading approved health messaging with your department's trusted saved voice. Then instantly reaching every community in 70+ languages — same clarity, same urgency, zero translation delay.

examples.label: Legal & Insurance Explainers
examples.description: Imagine uploading contract summaries and process diagrams with your advisor's saved voice. Then instantly generating visual breakdowns clients actually understand — before they sign or file a claim.

examples.label: Religious & Spiritual Teachings
examples.description: Imagine uploading sermon notes or sacred texts with your teacher's saved voice and presence. Then instantly publishing a consistent daily video series for global congregations — in 70+ languages.

audienceResonance.title: Audience Resonance Analysis
audienceResonance.description: Know your score before you invest in full renders. Target Audience Resonance scores Blueprint and script with actionable fixes — optimize every beat before Express and video generation.
bullets: Get real-time score, analysis, and recommendations
One-click fixes to resolve narrative weaknesses
Guided Edit to optimize Episodes, Blueprint, and Script

howWeScore.title: How Audience Resonance works
howWeScore.description: Audience Resonance combines narrative-structure analysis (clarity, pacing, emotional arc) with target-persona fit against the audience you define in Blueprint — not vanity metrics or post-publish retention data. Recommendations are tied to specific script sections so you can fix issues before Express or video generation.
bullets: Scores Blueprint and script against your stated target audience
Section-level recommendations — not a black-box “trust us” number
Actionable one-click fixes and guided edit — not just a grade
Run before heavy render spend, not after publish

express.title: Sceneflow Express
express.description: Auto-generate pre-vis, animatics, and video beats concurrently. Move from script to shareable preview in minutes — then approve Beat Frames before final F2V spend.

items.title: Express Pre-vis
items.desc: Review and share audio and video pre-vis in minutes vs hours.

items.title: Express Animatics
items.desc: Render full Ken Burns animatic scenes with high-end voiceovers instantly.

items.title: Express Video
items.desc: Orchestrate concurrent image and video generation—including native Veo extension chains for long dialogue beats.

preVisEngine.title: Pre-Visualization Engine
preVisEngine.subtitle: Generating a fully voiced, scored, 40-minute audio-visual animatic in 15 minutes is not a workflow step — that is the product. In Hollywood, pre-vis takes weeks and costs hundreds of thousands of dollars. SceneFlow scales it to minutes and pocket change.
preVisEngine.narrative: In the film industry, a storyboard is something you hand to a crew. Pre-vis is what you show studio executives, investors, and producers to get a project greenlit. SceneFlow elevates production from consumer creator tool to enterprise-grade pipeline asset.

pillars.title: The Ultimate Pitch Deck
pillars.description: Upload a script and walk into a pitch meeting with a fully voiced, scored, multi-language pre-vis plus hard data from Audience Resonance. Give creators a data-backed, audio-visual cheat code to get their movies made.

pillars.title: Fail-Fast Pre-Production
pillars.description: Traditional pre-vis requires 3D animators, storyboard artists, and voice actors for weeks. If a scene does not work, tens of thousands of dollars are wasted. Iterate in 15 minutes before a single camera crew member is hired.

pillars.title: Financier Analytics
pillars.description: Audience Resonance at upload appeals to the most risk-averse part of the industry: financiers. Show how pacing, tone, and dialogue align with target demographics before a single dollar is spent on production.

workflow.title: Script upload → greenlight-ready pre-vis
steps: Run Audience Resonance scoring on your target demographic
Generate Express Pre-vis — voiced, scored animatics in minutes
Share for stakeholder review before final video spend
